title: ripgit: A Git Server in a Single Durable Object
description: How ripgit implements a full git server with push, fetch, web UI, and issues — all inside one Cloudflare Durable Object with SQLite.

steps.title: One Durable Object = One Git Repo
steps.description: ripgit is a self-hosted git server on Cloudflare Workers. lib.rs routes each /:owner/:repo request to a Durable Object instance backed by SQLite — so every repository is an isolated, stateful process with its own database.

steps.title: Streaming Git Protocol
steps.description: git.rs handles push and fetch via Smart HTTP. Pack files are processed in two phases: pack.rs indexes entries with O(1) memory, then git.rs resolves deltas through a bounded 20 MB cache — keeping memory under Cloudflare's 128 MB limit even for large repos.

steps.title: Dual Storage with Delta Compression
steps.description: store.rs uses a dual-storage model: commits and trees are stored both parsed (for queries) and raw (for byte-perfect git fetch). Blobs use xpatch delta compression with keyframe intervals every 50 versions. Three FTS5 tables power full-text search across code and commits.

steps.title: Issues, PRs & Three-Way Merge
steps.description: issues.rs implements issues and pull requests in a unified table. PRs support real three-way merge: find the merge base via BFS, flatten both trees, union file-level changes, and create a proper merge commit — all in SQLite, no git binary needed.

steps.title: Server-Rendered Web UI, No Build Step
steps.description: web.rs generates all HTML via Rust format!() strings — no frontend framework, no static assets. Content negotiation in presentation.rs lets the same route serve HTML, JSON, or Markdown based on the Accept header.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: ~9,600 lines of Rust implement a complete git server: protocol handling, delta-compressed storage, full-text search, issues with real merges, and a server-rendered UI — all deployed as a single Cloudflare Worker.
